title: Karuppu Nila Song Lyrics En Aasai Machan
stanzas: <div>Karuppu Nilaa Song Lyrics En Aasai Machan

Karuppu nilaa
neethaan kalanguvdhu yaen.
thuli thuliyaai kanneer
vizhuvadhu yaen.(2)

chinna maane maanguyile
un manasula enna kurai.
peththa aaththaa pol iruppen
indha boomiyil vazhum vara.

ettu dhisai yaavum
katti arasaala
vandha rasaa neethaane.

Karuppu nilaa
neethaan kalanguvdhu yaen.
thuli thuliyaai kanneer
vizhuvadhu yaen

paththu maasam maddiyendhi
peththedutha magarasi.
pachcha pulla unnavittu
ponathenni azhuthaayaa.

maaman vandhu enaikaakka
naanum vandhu unai thaakka
naam virumbum inbam ellaam
naalai varum namakkaaga.

kaalam ulla kaalam
vazhum indha paasam.
poo vizhi imai moodiye
chinna poove kannurangu.

Karuppu nilaa
neethaan kalanguvdhu yaen.
thuli thuliyaai kanneer
vizhuvadhu yaen

vanna vanna mugam kaatti
vaanavillin niram kaatti
chinna chinna mazhalai pesi
chithirampol magane vaa

sembaruthi malar pole
sokka velli mani pole.
kannam rendum minna
minna kanmaniye
madimael vaa

paattu thamizh paattu
paada adha kaettu
aadidum vilaiyaadidum
thanga thaerey neethaane.

Karuppu nilaa
neethaan kalanguvdhu yaen.
thuli thuliyaai kanneer
vizhuvadhu yaen.(2)

en maane maanguyile
un manasula enna kurai.
peththa aaththaa pol iruppen
indha boomiyil vazhum vara.

ettu dhisai yaavum
katti arasaala
vandha rasaa neethaane.

Karuppu nilaa
neethaan kalanguvdhu yaen.
thuli thuliyaai kanneer
vizhuvadhu yaen

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of Karuppu Nila?

The song explores themes of longing and emotional turmoil, capturing the essence of unfulfilled desires.

Who composed the music for En Aasai Machan?

The music for the movie was composed by Deva.

Who sang Karuppu Nila?

The song was sung by the renowned playback singer K.S. Chithra.

When was En Aasai Machan released?

The movie was released in the year 1994.

What emotions does Karuppu Nila evoke?

The song evokes feelings of melancholy and nostalgia, resonating with anyone who has experienced unrequited love.
enrichedMetadata.summary: Karuppu Nila is a poignant melody from the movie En Aasai Machan, capturing the deep emotions of longing and heartache. The song's lyrics reflect the inner turmoil of a lover questioning the reasons behind their tears and sorrow. With K.S. Chithra's soulful rendition and Deva's haunting composition, the song beautifully conveys the pain of unfulfilled love. It evokes a sense of nostalgia and melancholy, making it a timeless classic in Tamil cinema.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Karuppu Nila from En Aasai Machan is a hauntingly beautiful melody that captures the essence of longing and nostalgia. With its soulful lyrics and mesmerizing tune, this song is a timeless classic in Tamil cinema. Experience the magic of K.S. Chithra's voice combined with Deva's enchanting music that makes this track unforgettable.
stanzaMeanings: Why are you, the black moon, troubled? Why are tears falling drop by drop?
Little deer, little cuckoo, what's bothering your heart? I will be like a mother to you as long as I live on this earth.
You are the king who came to rule all eight directions.
Why are you, the black moon, troubled? Why are tears falling drop by drop?
The queen born after ten months, why did you leave your little child and go away crying?
Uncle came to protect me, I came to support you. All the happiness we want will come to us tomorrow.
As long as time exists, this love will live. Close your flower-like eyes, little flower, and sleep.
Why are you, the black moon, troubled? Why are tears falling drop by drop?
Showing colorful faces, showing rainbow colors, speaking little baby words, come like a picture, son.
Like a hibiscus flower, like a silver bell, your cheeks are shining, come to my lap, dear.
Sing Tamil songs, listening to them, you will dance and play, you are the golden chariot.
Why are you, the black moon, troubled? Why are tears falling drop by drop?
My dear, little cuckoo, what's bothering your heart? I will be like a mother to you as long as I live on this earth.
You are the king who came to rule all eight directions.
Why are you, the black moon, troubled? Why are tears falling drop by drop?
